title: A door lock and a control device for the door lock
abstract: The present application relates to a door lock and a control apparatus thereof. The control apparatus includes a control circuit, including a connecting end, a control end, a common end, a switch apparatus, and an electronic driving apparatus. A first current circuit is formed between the connecting end and the common end through the switch apparatus. A second current circuit is formed between the control end and the common end through the electronic driving apparatus. The switch apparatus is turned on and turned off to control the first current circuit to be connected or disconnected. The electronic driving apparatus is used to drive a pin apparatus to turn on or turn off the switch apparatus. The pin apparatus has a tail end and a top end and can move toward the switch apparatus in a longitudinal direction and enable the switch apparatus to turn off. A direction-changing apparatus is used to enable, when a force applied to the pin apparatus in a transverse direction exceeds a predetermined value, the pin apparatus to move toward the switch apparatus in the longitudinal direction. In the door lock and the control apparatus thereof provided in the present application, a sensor is not used, so that changes made to an existing structure can be as few as possible, and when a door of an electrical appliance is forced open, a working circuit of the electrical appliance can be cut off in time to stop the operation of a machine.
claims:
Claims 1. A door lock, comprising: a switch apparatus; a pin apparatus, the pin apparatus being capable of moving in a first direction toward the switch apparatus or away from the switch apparatus; a direction-changing apparatus, the direction-changing apparatus being used to enable, when a force is applied to the pin apparatus in a second direction, the pin apparatus to move in the first direction toward the switch apparatus, to contact the switch apparatus to turn off the switch apparatus. 2. The door lock according to claim 1, wherein a top end and a tail end are disposed on the pin apparatus, and the direction-changing apparatus is disposed at the tail end of the pin apparatus or near the tail end. 3. The door lock according to claim 1, wherein the first direction is a longitudinal direction, and the second direction is a transverse direction. 4. The door lock according to claim 1, wherein the direction-changing apparatus is used to enable, when the force applied to the pin apparatus in the second direction exceeds a predetermined value, the pin apparatus to move in the first direction toward the switch apparatus, to contact the switch apparatus to turn off the switch apparatus. 5. The door lock according to claim 2, wherein a slider, the slider having a slider locking position and a slider release position, and being capable of moving reciprocally in the second direction between the slider locking position and the slider release position, where the slider applies a force to the pin apparatus in the second direction; the slider has a locking hole, the pin apparatus also has a pin apparatus locking position and a pin apparatus release position, the pin apparatus is inserted in the locking hole when moving to 

 the pin apparatus locking position to lock the slider in the slider locking position. 6. The door lock according to claims 5, wherein the switch apparatus comprises: a pair of elastic sheets, the elastic sheets comprising a dynamic elastic sheet and a static elastic sheet, wherein the top end of the pin apparatus is connected to the dynamic elastic sheet; when the pin apparatus is in the pin apparatus locking position, the pair of elastic sheets are in contact with each other, to enable the switch apparatus to turn on; and when the pin apparatus is in the pin apparatus release position, the pin apparatus separates the pair of elastic sheets, to enable the switch apparatus to turn off. 7. The door lock according to claim 5, wherein the direction-changing apparatus comprises a separable portion disposed at the tail end; the separable portion and the pin apparatus have an integrated structure; and the separable portion is arranged to lock the slider when the pin apparatus is in the pin apparatus locking position, and to be cut off when the slider is unlocked under a particular force, to enable an inclined surface to be formed at the tail end of the pin apparatus. 8. The door lock according to claim 7, wherein the length of the separable portion is less than the lateral width of the pin apparatus, to reduce the strength of the separable portion, to enable the separable portion to be cut off by a side wall of the locking hole to form the inclined surface. 9. The door lock according to claim 8, wherein the separable portion is disposed at two ends or in a middle position of the pin apparatus in a lateral width direction. 10. The door lock according to claim 5, further comprising: a cam, a lock hook being disposed on the cam, wherein when the cam rotates around a rotating shaft, the lock hook is capable of being locked at a door hook on a door of an electrical appliance; the slider is capable of abutting the cam to lock the cam when being in the slider locking 

 position; and the rotation of the cam is capable of driving the slider to move reciprocally between the slider locking position and the slider release position. 1 1. A control apparatus of a door lock, comprising: a control circuit, the control circuit comprising a switch apparatus; a pin apparatus, the pin apparatus being capable of moving in a longitudinal direction toward the switch apparatus and being capable of enabling the pin apparatus to be in contact with the switch apparatus, to enable the switch apparatus to turn off; and a mechanical driving apparatus, wherein the pin apparatus is capable of being driven by the mechanical driving apparatus to enable the pin apparatus to move in the longitudinal direction toward the switch apparatus, to enable the pin apparatus to be in contact with the switch apparatus, to enable the switch apparatus to turn off. 12. The control apparatus according to claim 11, further comprising: a direction-changing apparatus, the pin apparatus passes through the direction-changing apparatus, to enable, when a transverse force is applied to the pin apparatus, the pin apparatus to move in the longitudinal direction toward the switch apparatus, to enable the pin apparatus to be in contact with the switch apparatus, to enable the switch apparatus to turn off. 13. The control apparatus according to claim 12, wherein the direction-changing apparatus enables, when the transverse force applied to the pin apparatus is greater than or equal to a predetermined value, the pin apparatus to move in the longitudinal direction toward the switch apparatus, to enable the pin apparatus to be in contact with the switch apparatus, to enable the switch apparatus to turn off. 14. The control apparatus according to claim 12, further comprising: an electronic driving apparatus, where a driving signal for starting the electronic driving apparatus is an electronic signal, to drive the pin apparatus; and the mechanical driving apparatus is initially driven by a mechanical force, to drive the pin 

 apparatus. 15. The control apparatus according to claim 14, wherein the control circuit further comprises a connecting end, a control end, and a common end, wherein a first current circuit is formed between the connecting end and the common end through the switch apparatus, and a second current circuit is formed between the control end and the common end through the electronic driving apparatus; the pin apparatus is driven by the electronic driving apparatus to enable the pin apparatus to move in the longitudinal direction toward the switch apparatus, to enable the pin apparatus to be in contact with the switch apparatus, to enable the switch apparatus to turn off; the first current circuit and the second current circuit are connected to the common end through a common connecting point; the connecting end is capable of being serially connected to a power supply through an electric motor in the first current circuit; the control end is capable of being serially connected to the power supply through the electronic driving apparatus in the second current circuit; and the common end is connected to the ground of the power supply; the switch apparatus is capable of being tumed on or tumed off, the switch apparatus is turned on or tumed off to control the first current circuit to be connected or disconnected, to enable the electric motor to be connected to or disconnected from the power supply in the first current circuit. 16. The control apparatus according to claim 15, wherein when the electronic driving apparatus is started, the electronic driving apparatus is capable of driving the pin apparatus, to enable the pin apparatus to move in the longitudinal direction toward the switch apparatus. 17. The control apparatus according to claim 16, wherein the electronic driving apparatus comprises a coil and an iron core, and when the electronic 

 driving apparatus is connected to the second current circuit, the coil enables the iron core to move under the effect of an electromagnetic force, to drive the pin apparatus to make a longitudinal movement; and a convex shoulder is disposed on the pin apparatus, and the iron core drives the convex shoulder through a self-locking block, to drive the pin apparatus to move. 18. The control apparatus according to claim 17, wherein the driving signal is an excitation pulse, and each excitation pulse is capable of enabling the iron core to move once, to drive the self -locking block to move once; and the self-locking block has a locked state and a released state, and the self-locking block is configured to be switched between the locked state and the released state once when moving once. 19. The control apparatus according to claim 15, wherein the switch apparatus comprises a movable elastic sheet and a fixed elastic sheet, the pin apparatus has a tail end and a top end, and the top end of the pin apparatus is capable of being in contact with the movable elastic sheet; a movable contact is disposed at a distal end of the movable elastic sheet, a fixed contact is disposed at a distal end of the fixed elastic sheet, when the movable contact is in contact with the fixed contact, the switch apparatus is turned on, and the first current circuit is connected, and when the movable contact is separated from the fixed contact, the switch apparatus is turned off, and the first current circuit is disconnected. 20. The control apparatus according to claim 19, wherein the direction-changing apparatus is disposed at the tail end of the pin apparatus, and comprises a separable portion; and when a transverse force applied to the tail end of the pin apparatus is greater than or equal to a predetermined value, the separable portion is capable of being separated from the tail end to enable an inclined surface to be formed at the tail end of the pin apparatus, and the pin apparatus is capable of being pushed by a transverse force applied to the inclined surface to make a 

longitudinal movement.
